settings.title: Meet Dana, Who Keeps Our Cellar in Order
settings.excerpt: She tastes every bottle before it reaches your table, and she has opinions about temperature.
settings.body:
Most of what makes Corkwell work happens before you sit down, and a lot of it is Dana's doing. She manages the cellar, logs every delivery, and decides what comes off the list.

Dana started with us pouring weekend shifts and now tastes through forty-odd samples a month. Her rule is simple: if she would not pour it for a friend, it does not go on.

What keeping the cellar really involves

The job is far more than stacking bottles. Dana tracks what is selling and what is sitting, rotates stock so nothing is forgotten in a back corner, and notes the day each case arrives. When a wine starts to fade or stops earning its place, she pulls it before you ever notice. The reason the by-the-glass list feels reliable is that someone is quietly minding it every single week.

She is also the reason a few of our regulars now ask for things slightly cooler than they used to. Temperature is her quiet crusade, and once you taste the difference it is hard to unlearn.

A few of Dana's rules

  • Taste every sample before it is allowed near a table.
  • Serve reds cooler than most places do, around sixty degrees.
  • If she would not pour it for a friend, it does not make the list.

Her current obsession is serving reds a touch cooler than most places do, around sixty degrees. Order the Loire cabernet franc and you will see what she means. A few minutes out of a cool cellar, rather than a warm room, and the wine tastes fresher and more lifted.
